messages.content: 污染物扩散至催化剂表面 翻译
Pollutants diffuse to the surface of the catalyst.
但是论述的不够完全

好的，以下是更完整的翻译：

当污染物在化学反应过程中产生时，它们可能会通过扩散的方式到达催化剂表面，从而影响催化剂的性能。这可能会导致催化剂的活性减少或削弱其选择性，因为污染物可以占据催化剂表面上的活性位点，从而阻碍反应物分子的吸附和反应。因此，为了确保催化剂的长期稳定性和高效性，必须采取有效措施防止污染物的扩散到催化剂表面。
翻译 “但是论述的不够完全”
The description is not fully complete.
翻译 “这样论述有点过于绝对”
The statement is a bit too absolute.
翻译 “图中纯水和石化废水的图标标反了"
The icons for pure water and petrochemical wastewater are reversed in the figure.
翻译 "降低扩散层提高极限电流密度"
Reduce the diffusion layer to increase the limiting current density.
翻译 ”在工艺回流液中"
In the process refluxing liquid.
润色优化一下以下表达 “How to overcome the influence of the influent concentration change during the experiment or in making the conclusion"
How to mitigate the impact caused by variations in influent concentration during experiments or when drawing conclusions.

优化润色以下表达" Improving hydraulic contact is to reduce the diffusion layer in electrochemical reaction. Thus, the organic matters transfer should also be important in the process not only the ozone, not to mention the changes of the components into small size in the reflux"

Enhancing hydraulic contact involves minimizing the thickness of the diffusion layer during electrochemical reactions. Therefore, during the process, the transfer of organic matter should not be overlooked in addition to ozone. Additionally, changes in components during refluxing into smaller sizes should also be considered important.
优化润色以下表达 What is the dosage of O3 in g-O3/g-TOC in this study? For a better comparison with the cited references.
What is the gaseous ozone dosage in g-O3/g-TOC used in this study? This will allow for better comparisons with the references cited.
优化润色 The water temperature should be given exactly since this value determined the reaction process indeed and could vary from ambient temperature
The exact water temperature should be provided since it significantly impacts the reaction process and may vary from ambient temperatures.

优化润色it is noticed that the COD in influent decreased after the point, why this happened? Did the water components changed? How to mitigate the impact caused by variations in influent concentration during experiments or when drawing conclusions.

It is observed that the COD in the influent decreased after this point. Why did this occur? Did the components in the water change? Moreover, how can the impact resulting from variations in influent concentration be minimized when conducting experiments or drawing conclusions?
